question_text: Which of the following best describes the relationship between mass and weight?

options.label: A
options.text: Mass and weight are identical quantities measured in the same units

options.label: B
options.text: Weight is the gravitational force acting on an object's mass

options.label: C
options.text: Mass depends on location, but weight remains constant

options.label: D
options.text: Weight is a measure of the amount of matter in an object

correct_answer: Weight is the gravitational force acting on an object's mass
explanation: Weight is the force that gravity exerts on an object because of its mass. It is measured in newtons (N), while mass is measured in kilograms (kg) and stays the same everywhere. Weight changes depending on the gravitational field strength of different locations.
